title: env enter command
description:
Implement 'panopoly env enter [-e/--env <env>] [--action run|exec] <projname>' to place the user in a project's runtime shell.

What needs to be done:
- Default env is 'host' if -e/--env not given
- --action defaults to 'run'; accepts 'exec' as alternative
- For host environment (no image in config):
  - cd to env/<env>/run/<proj>/ and exec a shell (e.g. $SHELL) so direnv picks up the .envrc
- For container environment (image in [env.<envname>] config):
  - --action run: run 'podman run -it -v <root>/project:/project:ro -v <root>/source:/source:ro -v <root>/env/<env>:/env:rw <image> <shell>'
  - --action exec: run 'podman exec -it <container_name_or_id> <shell>' (container name defaults to env name)
- Tests: CLI test for host path verifying the shell exec command constructed; mock podman for container path
acceptance_criteria: env enter constructs correct shell/podman command for both host and container cases; --action flag works; tests pass

title: config capture command
description:
Implement 'panopoly config capture [-o outfile] [<what>...]' to inspect a panopoly area and emit TOML.

What needs to be done:
- Walk the panopoly area to discover: bare repos in source/, projects under project/, environments under env/
- Optionally narrow to specific sub-paths given as positional <what> arguments (e.g. 'source/', 'project/projX/', 'env/host/')
- Emit TOML sections representing what was found:
  - [source.<name>] with url from bare repo remote origin
  - [project.<name>] with sources list
  - [env.<name>] with image (if detectable), spack (if symlink), projects list
- Output to stdout or -o/--output file
- Tests: create a temp panopoly area with known structure; verify TOML output matches expected sections
acceptance_criteria: config capture emits valid TOML reflecting discovered area; narrowing by path works; tests pass

title: env add command
description:
Implement 'panopoly env add <envname> [<projname>...]' to create a named runtime environment skeleton.

What needs to be done:
- Create env/<env>/ skeleton: views/, build/, run/ subdirectories
- Optional --spack=/path/to/spack: create a symlink env/<env>/spack -> /path/to/spack
- For each relevant project (all known projects, or narrowed by explicit <projname> args):
  - Create env/<env>/views/<proj>/ (or symlink to existing view dir or Spack environment view path if --view option or config provides one)
  - Create env/<env>/build/<proj>/<repo>/ for each repo in project/<proj>/src/
  - Create env/<env>/run/<proj>/ directory
  - Generate env/<env>/run/<proj>/.envrc:
    - source_up or source to include project/<proj>/.envrc (generic relative path)
    - PANOPOLY_PREFIX set to env/<env>/views/<proj>/ (absolute, derived from fs context)
    - load_prefix $PANOPOLY_PREFIX  (direnv built-in, user must have direnv installed)
- Generate env/<env>/.envrc:
  - PANOPOLY_SPACK pointing to spack/ subdir (if spack present)
  - PATH_add $PANOPOLY_SPACK/bin (direnv built-in)
- Idempotent throughout
- If [env.<envname>] section exists in config, use image, spack, and other fields
- Tests: CLI test with temp filesystem; verify directory structure and .envrc content
acceptance_criteria: env add creates full skeleton; .envrc files have correct content; spack symlink created when --spack given; tests pass

title: project add command
description:
Implement 'panopoly project add <projectname> [<sourcename>...]' to create a project worktree area.

What needs to be done:
- Create project/<proj>/src/ directory
- For each relevant bare repo in source/ (all, or narrowed by explicit <sourcename> args): run 'git worktree add project/<proj>/src/<repo> <branch>' where branch defaults to the bare repo's HEAD; the repo name is the bare repo basename minus .git
- Generate project/<proj>/.envrc:
  - PANOPOLY_PROJECT: derived from directory name (generic discovery, not hardcoded)
  - PANOPOLY_WORKTREES: colon-separated absolute paths of all src/* worktrees
  - PANOPOLY_WORKTREE_<SRC>: absolute path to each worktree (uppercased src name)
  - .envrc should use relative-to-absolute expansion so it is generic
- Idempotent: skip already-existing worktrees and .envrc
- If [project.<projectname>] exists in config, use it for additional details
- Tests: CLI test creating a project from a local bare repo fixture; verify worktree exists; verify .envrc content is correct
acceptance_criteria: project add creates worktree at correct path; .envrc sets all three variable types correctly; tests pass

title: source add command
description:
Implement 'panopoly source add <giturl>' to add a bare git repo to the panopoly source/ area.

What needs to be done:
- Derive repo name from the git URL (strip trailing .git if present, take basename)
- Run 'git clone --bare <giturl> source/<name>.git'
- Idempotent: if source/<name>.git already exists, skip with a log message
- Tests: CLI-level test using Click test runner with a local bare repo as the URL; verify bare clone created at correct path
acceptance_criteria: source add clones a bare repo into source/<name>.git; re-running is a no-op; tests pass

title: init command
description:
Implement 'panopoly init [dir]' to bootstrap a new panopoly area.

What needs to be done:
- Create .panopoly/ marker directory (empty for now)
- Create source/, project/, env/ skeleton directories
- Accept optional directory argument (default: current directory)
- Support --layout <name> option: look up [layout.<name>] section in config; that section may have: sources (list of git remote URLs -> delegate to source add), projects (list of project names -> delegate to project add), envs (list of env names -> delegate to env add)
- All operations idempotent (safe to re-run)
- Tests: CLI-level test using Click test runner; verify directory structure created; verify --layout dispatches correctly with a mock config
acceptance_criteria: 'panopoly init' creates .panopoly/, source/, project/, env/; re-running is safe; --layout with a config section triggers appropriate sub-commands; tests pass

title: Core CLI infrastructure
description:
Build the foundational layer that all panopoly subcommands share.

What needs to be done:
- Click app entry point in src/panopoly/__init__.py with top-level --root, --log-level, --log-file options
- Root detection: walk up directories looking for .panopoly/ marker; --root overrides; raise a clear error if not found (except for init which creates it)
- Config loading: merge ~/.config/panopoly/config.toml (user) with .panopoly/config.toml (local); local sections override user sections by section name; expose a config object passed down to subcommands
- Logging setup: default stderr, default level INFO, controlled by CLI options
- Shared path helpers: functions that derive canonical paths (source/, project/<proj>/src/<repo>/, env/<env>/...) from panopoly root
- Tests: unit tests for root detection walk-up logic and config merge behavior
acceptance_criteria: Root auto-detected from subdirectory; local config overrides user config section-for-section; --root flag works; logging directed to stderr by default; all shared helpers covered by unit tests
close_reason: Click CLI group with --root/--log-level/--log-file, PanopolyRoot with path helpers, find_root() walk-up, load_config() deep-merge of user+local TOML. 25 tests all pass.

title: Panopoly initial implementation
description: Implement the initial working version of the panopoly CLI tool. Panopoly manages a development area consisting of bare git repos (source/), project worktrees (project/), and named run environments (env/). The CLI uses Click, with logging, config file support, and direnv .envrc generation. This epic covers the core infrastructure plus all first-iteration subcommands: init, source add, project add, env add, config capture, and env enter.
